rationale: The citing paper uses Homburg et al. (2015) as a key prior empirical benchmark on how firm-generated content relates to subsequent user sentiment, first in the motivation section and then in its comparative literature table. It explicitly positions its own contribution against that study's aggregate-volume focus by arguing for conversation-level content features and moderation by preceding user sentiment. The citation is also revisited in the discussion to explain conflicting prior findings and clarify boundary conditions. This is a meaningful framing and positioning role, not a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Homburg et al. (2015) by building on the same core FGC-to-UGC sentiment question but moving from aggregate engagement volume in online communities to a richer conversation-level model with multiple FGC elements and sentiment-contingent effects in Twitter threads.
